sourceMovie.title: Brotherhood of Blades
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Brotherhood of Blades (2014) stuck because it commits to its romance premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Lu Yang's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The romance and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Supercross
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Brotherhood of Blades clicked for you, Supercross (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and action, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: A Writer's Odyssey
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Brotherhood of Blades clicked for you, A Writer's Odyssey (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Brotherhood of Blades?
faq.answer: Try Supercross, A Writer's Odyssey, Dragon—each shares DNA with Brotherhood of Blades while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Brotherhood of Blades?
faq.answer: Brotherhood of Blades (2014) is best described as Romance, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Brotherhood of Blades part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Brotherhood of Blades Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
